ROBERT GOTTSCHALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Punitive Damages And Other Language From Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint For Survival, Wrongful Death - Asbestos
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, September 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant John Crane Inc.¿s motion to strike is off calendar as moot given the ruling on the demurrer and the fact that plaintiffs dismissed their claim for punitive damages. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT GOTTSCHALL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, September 27, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant John Crane Inc.¿s demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. It is sustained on the grounds that plaintiffs failed to identify the product associated with defendant as required by Bockrath. Plaintiffs are granted 10 days leave to amend. The demurrer is overruled as to defendant¿s Boeken argument that there is no difference between the survival and wrongful death negligence and strict liability causes of action. Boeken is inapplicable. C.C.P. §§ 377.20 and 377.60 lay out the survival and wrongful death statutes. § 377.60 creates a new cause of action in favor of the decedent¿s heirs. (Horwich v. Superior Court (1999) 21 Cal.4th 272, 283). = (220/HEK)
